chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَنْ صَامَ فِي السَّفَرِ بِجَهَالَةٍ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 49 – The one who Fasts during the journey out of ignorance

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/329)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/329)
text: إِذَا سَافَرَ الرَّجُلُ فِي شَهْرِ رَمَضَانَ أَفْطَرَ وَ إِنْ صَامَهُ بِجَهَالَةٍ لَمْ يَقْضِهِ .

en.hubeali: Safwan Bin Yahya, from Abdullah Bin Muskan, from Lays Al Murady,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘When the man travels during a Month of Ramazan, he should break (not Fast); and if he were to Fast it out of ignorance, he would not make up for it’.
en.sarwar: 3. From Safwan ibn Yahya has narrated from ‘Abd Allah ibn Muskan from Layth al-Muradiy who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If one travels in the month of Ramadan he must stop fasting; but if he fasts because of ignorance, he is not required to complete its Qada’ (compensatory fast).”
